title: ferray-ma: MaskedArray is not interoperable with ferray Array functions
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's MaskedArray subclasses ndarray, so any function that accepts ndarray also accepts MaskedArray (with mask propagation via __array_ufunc__). ferray's MaskedArray is a separate struct with no trait relationship to Array. You can't pass a MaskedArray to ferray-stats::sum, ferray-ufunc::sin, or ferray-linalg::solve. Users must extract .data(), operate, then reconstruct the mask. NumPy's design where MaskedArray IS-A ndarray is the key feature of numpy.ma. In Rust, this could be modeled via: (1) a trait that both Array and MaskedArray implement, (2) AsRef<Array> on MaskedArray, or (3) making ufunc/stats functions generic over a MaskAware trait.
